output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 数据模型定义
// ============================================

class TestObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Create observer relationship and test notification
 *
 * @param observerName Name for the observer node
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return std::pair<bool, int> Pair of whether observer was notified and call count
 */
std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    REACTION_REGISTER_THREAD();
    
    // Create observer nodes
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Add observer relationship: target observes observer
    observerGraph.addObserver(target, observer);
    
    // Notify target of change
    target->notify(true);
    
    // Return notification status
    return {observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount()};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test observer node naming functionality
 *
 * @param name Name to set for the observer node
 * @return std::string The retrieved name from the observer graph
 */
std::string test_observer_naming(const std::string& name) {
    // Create an observer node
    auto observer_node = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    
    // Get the observer graph instance
    auto& observer_graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Set the node name
    observer_graph.setName(observer_node, name);
    
    // Retrieve and return the node name
    return observer_graph.getName(observer_node);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestChainedObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestChainedObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test chained observer relationships
 *
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @param observer1Name Name for the first observer node
 * @param observer2Name Name for the second observer node (observes observer1)
 * @return std::pair<int, bool> Pair of total call count and whether chain notification worked correctly
 */
std::pair<int, bool> test_chained_observers(const std::string& targetName, 
                                            const std::string& observer1Name, 
                                            const std::string& observer2Name) {
    // 1. Create target node using TestChainedObserver(targetName)
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(targetName);
    
    // 2. Create first observer node using TestChainedObserver(observer1Name)
    auto observer1 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer1Name);
    
    // 3. Create second observer node using TestChainedObserver(observer2Name)
    auto observer2 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer2Name);
    
    // 4. Get observer graph instance using reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance()
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 5. Add observer relationships: target observes observer1, observer1 observes observer2
    observerGraph.addObserver(target, observer1);
    observerGraph.addObserver(observer1, observer2);
    
    // 6. Notify target node using valueChanged(true)
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    // 7. Check if both observers were notified (m_changed flag set to true)
    bool observer1Notified = observer1->wasChanged();
    bool observer2Notified = observer2->wasChanged();
    
    // 8. Calculate total call count (sum of observer1 and observer2 call counts)
    int totalCalls = observer1->getCallCount() + observer2->getCallCount();
    
    // 9. Verify chain notification: observer2 should have been notified through observer1 -> true if observer2 was called
    bool chainVerification = observer2Notified && observer1Notified;
    
    // 10. Return pair with total calls and chain verification result
    return std::make_pair(totalCalls, chainVerification);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

instruction:
Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_batch_execution` function.

The function should:
1. Create reactive variables
2. Create variable to track recomputation count
3. Create calculation
4. Perform non-batch updates
5. Record recompute count after non-batch updates
6. Create batch context
7. Execute batch
8. Record recompute count after batch updates
9. Return final sum and counts

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test batch execution reduces recomputation count
 *
 * @param initialA Initial value for variable a
 * @param initialB Initial value for variable b
 * @param nonBatchA Value to set a without batching
 * @param nonBatchB Value to set b without batching
 * @param batchA Value to set a within batch
 * @param batchB Value to set b within batch
 * @return std::tuple<int, int, int> {final sum, count after non-batch updates, count after batch updates}
 */
std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                               int initialB,
                                               int nonBatchA,
                                               int nonBatchB,
                                               int batchA,
                                               int batchB) {}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

related functions.name: var
related functions.description: Create a reactive variable

related functions.name: calc
related functions.signature: auto (Fun &&, Args &&...) -> React<CalcExpr, ReturnType<Fun, Args...>, IV, TR>
related functions.description: Create a reactive calculation based on a function and its arguments

related functions.name: batch
related functions.description: Create a batch context to group reactive updates

related functions.name: value
related functions.description: Set the value of a reactive variable

related functions.name: get
related functions.description: Get the current value of the reactive calculation

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>
#include <cmath>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test filter trigger with conditional recomputation
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial sensor value
 * @param firstUpdate First update (expected to be within threshold)
 * @param secondUpdate Second update (expected to exceed threshold)
 * @param diffThreshold Minimum delta to trigger recomputation
 * @param levelThreshold Threshold to categorize HIGH vs NORMAL
 * @return std::tuple<std::string, int, int>
 *         {final status string, recompute count after first update, recompute count after second update}
 */
std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {
    using namespace reaction;
    
    // 1. Create reactive variable (sensor)
    auto sensor = var(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Create variables to track recomputation count and last processed value
    auto recomputeCount = var(0);
    auto lastProcessed = var(initialValue);
    
    // 3. Create filter-triggered calculation
    auto statusCalc = calc([&]() {
        double current = sensor();
        double last = lastProcessed();
        
        // Update last processed value and count if threshold is met
        if (std::abs(current - last) >= diffThreshold) {
            lastProcessed.value(current);
            recomputeCount.value(recomputeCount() + 1);
        }
        
        // Determine status based on level threshold
        return current > levelThreshold ? "HIGH" : "NORMAL";
    });
    
    // 4. Apply filter to calculation using .filter() method
    statusCalc.filter([diffThreshold](double current, double last) {
        return std::abs(current - last) >= diffThreshold;
    }, sensor, lastProcessed);
    
    // 5. Update sensor with first value
    sensor.value(firstUpdate);
    
    // 6. Record recompute count after first update
    int countAfterFirst = recomputeCount();
    
    // 7. Update sensor with second value
    sensor.value(secondUpdate);
    
    // 8. Record recompute count after second update
    int countAfterSecond = recomputeCount();
    
    // 9. Get final status
    std::string finalStatus = statusCalc();
    
    // 10. Return final status and counts
    return std::make_tuple(finalStatus, countAfterFirst, countAfterSecond);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

We are to implement the function `test_last_handle_restore` according to the specifications.
 Steps:
 1. Create a reactive variable (using `reaction::var`) with the initial value.
 2. Create a calculation (using `reaction::calc`) with the LastHandle policy. The calculation should depend on the reactive variable.
 3. Get the initial result of the calculation.
 4. Update the source variable to a new value.
 5. Get the updated result of the calculation.
 6. Close the source node (the reactive variable) using `close()`.
 7. Get the result after the source is closed (which should be the last known value due to LastHandle policy).
 8. Return a `LastHandleSnapshot` struct containing the three results.

 Note: The LastHandle policy is used in the calculation. We must specify the policy when creating the calculation.

 However, note that the `calc` function template has a template parameter for the invalidation policy and trigger policy.
 The LastHandle policy is an invalidation policy. We can specify it as the second template argument (the first is the trigger policy, which defaults to ChangeTrig).

 But looking at the provided `calc` function signature:

 We see that the default invalidation policy is `KeepHandle`. We want to change it to `LastHandle`.

 So we will call `calc` with the second template argument set to `LastHandle`.

 Example:

 However, note that the function `calc` also takes a function and arguments. The function should return the value of the calculation.

 Let's assume we are going to create a calculation that simply returns the value of the reactive variable.

 Steps in code:

   auto calcExpr = reaction::calc<reaction::ChangeTrig, reaction::LastHandle>([&source]() { return source(); });

 Then we get the initial value of calcExpr by calling it: int initialResult = calcExpr();

 Then update the source: source.value(updatedValue);

 Then get the updated result: int updatedResult = calcExpr();

 Then close the source: source.close();

 Then get the result after close: int afterCloseResult = calcExpr();

 Then return LastHandleSnapshot{initialResult, updatedResult, afterCloseResult};

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ValiditySnapshot {
    bool firstValidBeforeClose;
    bool firstValidAfterClose;
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose;
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator bool and operator== for validity tracking.
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value stored inside the reactive variable
 * @return ValiditySnapshot Snapshot describing validity/equality before and after close
 */
inline ValiditySnapshot test_react_validity(int initialValue) {
    ValiditySnapshot snapshot;
    
    // 1. Create reactive variable
    auto source = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Create copy of source
    auto copy = source;
    
    // 3. Check validity before close
    snapshot.firstValidBeforeClose = static_cast<bool>(source) && static_cast<bool>(copy);
    
    // 4. Check equality before close
    snapshot.copiesEqualBeforeClose = (source == copy);
    
    // 5. Close source node
    source.close();
    
    // 6. Check validity after close
    snapshot.firstValidAfterClose = static_cast<bool>(source);
    
    // 7. Check equality after close with exception handling
    try {
        snapshot.copiesEqualAfterClose = (source == copy);
        snapshot.equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = false;
    } catch (...) {
        snapshot.copiesEqualAfterClose = false;
        snapshot.equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = true;
    }
    
    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct UnaryExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    bool logicalNotAfterFlip;
    int bitwiseNotValue;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate unary expression builders and operators on reactive values.
 *
 * This function showcases how low-level expression builder helpers (`make_binary_op`,
 * `make_unary_op`) and high-level unary operators (`-`, `!`, `~`) cooperate with the
 * `expr` factory to produce reactive expressions.
 *
 * @param a First input value participating in the sum expression
 * @param b Second input value participating in the sum expression
 * @return UnaryExpressionSnapshot Snapshot of expression outputs before/after updates
 */
inline UnaryExpressionSnapshot test_unary_expression_builders(int a, int b) {
    // 1. Create reactive variables
    auto var_a = reaction::var(a);
    auto var_b = reaction::var(b);
    
    // 2. Build negated sum expression: -(a + b)
    auto sum_expr = var_a + var_b;
    auto negated_sum_expr = -sum_expr;
    auto negated_sum_node = reaction::expr(negated_sum_expr);
    
    // 3. Create reactive boolean variable
    auto flag_var = reaction::var(true);
    
    // 4. Build logical NOT expression: !flag
    auto logical_not_expr = !flag_var;
    auto logical_not_node = reaction::expr(logical_not_expr);
    
    // 5. Create reactive integer variable
    auto num_var = reaction::var(5);
    
    // 6. Build bitwise NOT expression: ~num
    auto bitwise_not_expr = ~num_var;
    auto bitwise_not_node = reaction::expr(bitwise_not_expr);
    
    // 7. Get initial negated sum value
    int initial_negated_sum = negated_sum_node.get();
    
    // 8. Update variables to trigger recomputation
    var_a.value(10);
    var_b.value(20);
    flag_var.value(false);
    num_var.value(3);
    
    // 9. Get updated values
    int updated_negated_sum = negated_sum_node.get();
    bool logical_not_after_flip = logical_not_node.get();
    int bitwise_not_value = bitwise_not_node.get();
    
    // 10. Return unary expression snapshot
    return UnaryExpressionSnapshot{
        initial_negated_sum,
        updated_negated_sum,
        logical_not_after_flip,
        bitwise_not_value
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_thread_manager_force_register` function.

The function should:
1. Get thread manager instance
2. Reset thread manager state
3. Check thread safety before registration
4. Register main thread
5. Check thread safety after main thread registration
6. Create worker thread and register it
7. Wait for worker thread to complete
8. Check thread safety after worker thread registration
9. Return force register snapshot

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *
 * Collects observable state around calls to ThreadManager registration APIs.
 */
struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

/**
 * Function: test_thread_manager_force_register
 *
 * Demonstrates how registering multiple distinct threads causes
 * ThreadManager to enable thread safety.
 *
 * - We start from a clean ThreadManager state.
 * - The main thread registers itself and remains the sole thread.
 * - A worker thread then also registers, which should trigger
 *   multi-thread detection and enable thread safety.
 *
 * @return ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *         A snapshot of the thread-safety flag before any registration,
 *         after the main-thread registration, and after the worker-thread registration.
 */
inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register();

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: RawExpressionSnapshot
 *
 * Captures the values produced by raw expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) before and after updating
 * their reactive inputs.
 */
struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;

    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

/**
 * Function: test_raw_expression_builders
 *
 * Demonstrates how low-level expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) can be combined with
 * `expr` to build reactive calculations without using the higher
 * level `make_binary_op` / `make_unary_op` shortcuts.
 *
 * The function:
 * - Creates three reactive integers A, B, C.
 * - Builds sum = A + B via make_binary_expr.
 * - Builds negated = -(A + B) via make_unary_expr(make_binary_expr(...)).
 * - Builds chained = -(A + B) * C via nested make_* calls.
 * - Updates A and C and records all expression values before and after.
 *
 * @param a Initial value for the first input
 * @param b Initial value for the second input
 * @param c Initial value for the chaining multiplier
 * @return RawExpressionSnapshot
 *         Snapshot of sum / negated / chained values before and after updates.
 */
inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c);

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

 * @param b Initial value for the second input
 * @param c Initial value for the chaining multiplier
 * @return RawExpressionSnapshot
 *         Snapshot of sum / negated / chained values before and after updates.
 */
inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c);

}  // namespace test_reaction
